title: LedgerEntryPosted v1
description: Emitted by ledger-service (via outbox) when a double-entry posting has been recorded in the append-only ledger. Published to ledger.entries.v1.

schema_version.description: Monotonic integer version of this schema encoded as a string. Consumers MUST skip (and warn on) unknown versions.

message_id.description: Globally unique message identifier. Used as idempotency key by consumers (inbox/dedup pattern).

correlation_id.description: Propagated across all services in the same business transaction. Set once at the transaction boundary.

causation_id.description: message_id of the upstream message that caused this one. Omitted at the start of a causal chain.

occurred_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the event occurred (not published). Must include timezone (Z or offset).

entry_id.description: Unique identifier for this ledger entry. Immutable once posted.

payment_id.description: The payment this entry relates to. Absent for non-payment entries.

refund_id.description: The refund this entry relates to. Present only for refund postings.

merchant_id.description: Identifier of the merchant this posting belongs to.

posting_type.description: The business event that triggered this ledger entry.

lines.description: Exactly one debit and one credit leg. Amounts must balance across legs.

idempotency_key.description: Idempotency key for this posting. Consumers must deduplicate on this field. Typically <payment_id>:<posting_type>.

posted_at.description: Timestamp when the entry was recorded by the ledger service.
